local_validation.notes: Prompt guide passed and wellness reported deploy aligned at origin/main 898a5ca8. The focused multi-source band passed as a 3-kernel only host-I/O proof with verdict 65535 and 1 ok, 0 divergent. It now records route telemetry with source, route, path, start/end/elapsed ms from now_unix_ms, serve result, and request/wire/body byte counts; that telemetry must match the socket route trace before the source-data receipt is valid. It also records stream ack/replay with read cursor 1, ack cursor 3, replay cursor 1, total-after-cursor 3, window count 2, and deferred count 1; those fields must match the pressure receipt before the stream source-data receipt is valid. The real-source band passed with verdict 8191 after citation offsets were updated, and base summary-learning passed with verdict 262143 plus fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way.

ci_validation.notes: CI not run yet in this evidence record.

deploy_validation.summary: No deploy performed yet. This is a local Form stdlib and documentation slice.

phase_gate.gate: Cells and recipes can inspect route telemetry and stream ack/replay as native source-coordinate receipt gates before summary learning.
phase_gate.next: Move route telemetry from loopback socket proof into production front-door telemetry, and move stream ack/replay from read-window receipts into producer pacing against live backpressure.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: No public API behavior changed. Form-native summary source receipts now require route telemetry and stream ack/replay rows in the focused source-coordinate proof.
test_flows: The multi-source band drives a real local TCP request through kh-serve-listener and validates telemetry against socket trace bytes and route timing.
The stream lane reads real .fkb channel data and validates pressure, ack cursor, replay cursor, total-after-cursor, window count, and deferred count before learning.
The existing summary and real-source bands remain green.
e2e_validation.summary: Local Form proof passed; no public endpoint changed.
